item.title: TIMES SHOPPER | Kitchen appliances you can rely on
item.description: THE best moments at home often begin in the kitchen. It is where breakfast starts busy mornings, where favourite family recipes are shared, and where celebrations come alive around the dining table. Every memorable meal deserves appliances you can rely on. That is why Narhari proudly brings you the complete Baumatic and Fiesta cooking range collection, combining quality, performance, and style to
item.content:encoded:
THE best moments at home often begin in the kitchen. It is where breakfast starts busy mornings, where favourite family recipes are shared, and where celebrations come alive around the dining table.

Every memorable meal deserves appliances you can rely on. That is why Narhari proudly brings you the complete Baumatic and Fiesta cooking range collection, combining quality, performance, and style to suit every home and every cooking style.

Baumatic is designed for those who love premium cooking experiences. Its gas ranges deliver outstanding heat control, generous oven capacity, and modern European styling that adds elegance to any kitchen.

Whether you are roasting, baking, grilling, or preparing several dishes at once, Baumatic cooking ranges help you cook with confidence while achieving delicious results every time.

Cooking becomes even more enjoyable with Baumatic gas and ceramic hobs. Fast heating, responsive temperature control, and durable construction make everyday meal preparation smooth and efficient.

Their contemporary appearance blends beautifully with modern kitchen designs while providing the performance serious home cooks appreciate.

Complete the look with a Baumatic rangehood that keeps your kitchen fresh and comfortable.

Powerful extraction helps remove smoke, steam, and cooking odours, allowing you to enjoy a cleaner cooking environment every day.

Pair it with a Baumatic electric skillet to prepare everything from hearty breakfasts to family-sized meals with consistent cooking performance.

Outdoor entertaining also becomes effortless with Baumatic barbecue and planchas, giving you the freedom to grill delicious meals for family gatherings and weekend get-togethers. Finish your kitchen with Baumatic cookware that delivers reliable performance and even heat distribution, making every recipe easier to prepare.

Fiesta cooking ranges are built for busy households that value practicality without compromising quality.

Spacious ovens, dependable gas burners, and easy-to-use controls make daily cooking simple whether you are preparing a quick breakfast, school lunches, or a Sunday feast for the entire family.

Fiesta offers dependable performance that makes every meal easier to prepare.

The Fiesta gas and ceramic hob collection gives you the flexibility to cook several dishes simultaneously while maintaining excellent heat control.

Combined with Fiesta rangehoods, your kitchen stays cleaner and fresher throughout every cooking session, creating a more enjoyable place to spend time with family.

Every kitchen deserves appliances that work as hard as you do. Baumatic delivers premium performance with refined European styling, while Fiesta provides dependable everyday value that families can trust.

Together, they offer a complete cooking solution designed to inspire confidence every time you step into the kitchen.

Now is the perfect opportunity to upgrade your cooking experience.

Discover the complete range of Baumatic and Fiesta cooking appliances at Narhari and bring home quality that will serve your family for years to come.

Suva • Lautoka • Nadi • Ba • Yadua-Sigatoka • Rakiraki

• Nausori • Navua • Savusavu • Nakasi • Labasa.

🌐 Visit our website: www.narharifiji.com

Follow us for the latest deals, promos and product updates:

Facebook: www.facebook.com/narhari.fiji;

Instagram: www.instagram.com/narhari.fj;

TikTok: www.tiktok.com/@narhari_officialaccount;

Narhari – TikTok: www.tiktok.com/@narhari_officialaccount

Narhari – Quality solutions for your every room.

Right: Upgrade your kitchen today with Baumatic kitchen appliances.

© 2026 FijiTimes. All rights reserved.

item.title: TIMES SHOPPER | Wash you fruits and vegies just before you eat it
item.description: FRESH fruits and vegetables are an essential part of a healthy diet, providing the vitamins, minerals and nutrients our bodies need every day. Whether you’ve picked up colourful produce from your local supermarket, explored the weekend farmers’ market or harvested vegetables from your own garden, one simple step should never be overlooked before you enjoy them washing them properly.
item.content:encoded:
FRESH fruits and vegetables are an essential part of a healthy diet, providing the vitamins, minerals and nutrients our bodies need every day.

Whether you’ve picked up colourful produce from your local supermarket, explored the weekend farmers’ market or harvested vegetables from your own garden, one simple step should never be overlooked before you enjoy them washing them properly.

According to allrecipes.com, many people assume that a quick rinse is enough, while others rely on soaps or special fruit washes.

The good news is that cleaning your produce doesn’t have to be complicated. With a few simple techniques, you can remove dirt, reduce residues and enjoy your fresh food with confidence.

Before reaching your kitchen, fruits and vegetables travel a long journey.

They are grown in fields or orchards, handled during harvesting, transported to packing facilities, displayed in stores and often touched by many hands before ending up in your shopping basket.

During that journey, produce can collect dust, soil, bacteria and tiny traces of pesticides. Some fruits and vegetables are also coated with a thin layer of edible wax to help maintain freshness and protect them during transport.

Although these residues are generally considered safe within regulated limits, washing your produce helps remove surface dirt and unwanted particles while giving you peace of mind before eating or cooking.

One of the biggest mistakes people make is washing all their produce as soon as they get home from the supermarket.

While it may seem like a great way to save time, excess moisture can actually cause some fruits and vegetables to spoil more quickly.

Leafy greens, fresh herbs and berries are especially delicate. Washing them before storage can encourage mould growth and shorten their shelf life.

Instead, it’s best to wash fruits and vegetables just before you’re ready to use them.

If you prefer preparing snacks ahead of time, make sure the produce is thoroughly dried before returning it to the refrigerator.

For most fruits and vegetables, the simplest method is also the most effective.

Hold the produce under cool running water while gently rubbing the surface with your hands. This removes most dirt, dust and surface residues without the need for soap, detergent or chemical produce washes.

There’s no need to soak fruits and vegetables in bleach or cleaning solutions, as these products are not designed for food and may leave harmful residues behind.

For firm produce such as potatoes, carrots, cucumbers and apples, using a clean vegetable brush while rinsing under water can help remove stubborn dirt.

Certain fruits and vegetables require slightly different cleaning methods to ensure they are free from grit while staying fresh and intact.

Leeks are well known for trapping soil between their tightly packed layers. Trim off the roots and dark green tops, slice the leek, then place the pieces into a bowl of cold water.

Swish them gently with your hands and lift them out, leaving the grit behind at the bottom of the bowl.

Berries, including raspberries and blackberries, are extremely delicate. Place them in a colander and rinse gently under cool running water just before serving. Spread them on paper towels and gently pat dry to avoid bruising.

Leafy greens and fresh herbs should also be rinsed carefully under cool water. If they contain visible dirt or sand, soak them briefly in a bowl of cold water before lifting them out and draining them in a colander. A salad spinner is an excellent way to remove excess water and keep leaves crisp.

Mushrooms require a gentle touch. Quickly rinse them under running water while rubbing away any dirt with your fingers, then pat dry with a clean paper towel. Avoid soaking mushrooms, as they absorb water easily and can become soggy.

Making produce washing part of your daily routine is one of the easiest ways to support good food hygiene. It takes only a few minutes but helps remove dirt, bacteria and other unwanted particles before meals are prepared.

Pair clean produce with proper food handling by washing your hands before cooking, using clean chopping boards and storing fruits and vegetables correctly in the refrigerator.

Fresh fruits and vegetables are among the healthiest foods you can eat.

By giving them a quick but thorough wash before serving, you’ll enjoy every bite knowing your meals are as fresh, clean and safe as possible.

After all, great meals begin with fresh ingredients, and clean produce is the first step towards healthier eating.

Clean fruits and vegetables are more appealing to eat and prepare. Picture: GOOGLE

Whether you’ve picked up colourful produce from your local supermarket, explored the weekend farmers’ market or harvested vegetables from your own garden, one simple step should never be overlooked before you enjoy them — wash it properly. Pictures: GOOGLE

item.title: TIMES SHOPPER | VERE’S TAKE | Buy more food from the market
item.description: Bula vinaka everyone, buying more fresh food from the market is the wisest choice right now. After the budget discussions, fuel price concerns and rising household costs, perhaps it’s time to appreciate something we’ve always had, our local food. Tavioka, dalo, rourou, ota, bele, kumala, chilies, lemons, fresh fish, uto, bananas and tropical fruits aren’t just part of us, they’re the most affordab
item.content:encoded:
Bula vinaka everyone, buying more fresh food from the market is the wisest choice right now. After the budget discussions, fuel price concerns and rising household costs, perhaps it’s time to appreciate something we’ve always had, our local food.

Tavioka, dalo, rourou, ota, bele, kumala, chilies, lemons, fresh fish, uto, bananas and tropical fruits aren’t just part of us, they’re the most affordable and nutritious foods available. With imported goods becoming more expensive, one way or the other buying locally has never made more sense.

Every imported apple, orange, packet of frozen vegetables or processed snack has travelled thousands of kilometres before reaching supermarket shelves. Those transport costs eventually end up in consumers’ shopping baskets, and the food is not as fresh as what is available locally in our markets and roadside stalls.

Our locally grown food, reaches markets within a day or two of harvest.

Shopping locally can also stretch a family’s budget. A bundle of rourou or bele costs $2 to $3 far less than packaged salad greens that costs somewhere between $7 to $10 or more, while tavioka and dalo remain staple alternatives to imported rice or other processed foods.

Choosing local doesn’t mean sacrificing variety. Fiji’s cuisine reflects the country’s rich cultural heritage, blending Indigenous, Indo-Fijian, Chinese and European influences. From fresh fish and lovo to fish curry and roti, our everyday meals are proof that local ingredients create world-class dishes, more fresh and affordable.

Buying local food supports farmers, fishermen and market vendors who are navigating the same economic challenges as everyone else. Every dollar spent on local produce stays within Fiji’s economy, helping sustain rural livelihoods and strengthen food security.

Consumers can also reduce food waste by buying seasonal produce. Fruits and vegetables that are in season are generally fresher, more abundant and more affordable than imported alternatives. As households continue adjusting to the realities of the new Budget, one simple strategy stands out, fill your shopping bags with Fiji-grown food.

It’s often cheaper, healthier and fresher and every purchase supports our people and communities. In uncertain economic times, perhaps the smartest shopping decision isn’t finding the next big supermarket special.

It’s rediscovering the value that’s already growing in our own backyard.Healthline.com says buying and eating local food offers benefits that extend beyond the dinner table.

It supports healthier eating, strengthens local economies and reduces environmental impact, making it a smart choice for consumers and communities alike.

Fruits, vegetables and other products are harvested only a day or two before reaching the markets, allowing them to ripen naturally and develop better flavour.

Because they travel shorter distances, they spend less time in storage and generally arrive in better condition than imported produce.

Vitamins and antioxidants begin to decline once produce is harvested, particularly during transport and storage. Since locally grown food reaches consumers more quickly, it is more likely to retain its nutritional value.

Shopping at the market and buying directly from farmers also encourages shoppers to try seasonal and unique foods that may not be available in supermarkets.

It provides an opportunity to learn more about our farmers and develops a stronger connection and appreciation with our agriculture sector.

Choosing local food benefits the environment by reducing the need for long-distance transportation, which lowers greenhouse gas emissions and fuel consumption.

Many locally grown products are sold with little packaging, helping reduce plastic waste.

Supporting sustainable local farming helps protect farmland, biodiversity and natural ecosystems that come with economic benefits.

Money spent on local produce stays within the community, supporting farmers, creating jobs for labourers and strengthens businesses.

Buying directly from producers allows shoppers to appreciate the effort involved in growing local food.

Whether through farmers’ markets, roadside stalls or community supported agriculture programs, eating local is an easy way to enjoy fresher, healthier food.

Shop for more fresh food to improve your family’s dietary choices.

© 2026 FijiTimes. All rights reserved.

item.title: TIMES SHOPPER | Fiji Pearls head coach enjoys her shopping experience
item.description: MOVING to a new country comes with plenty of adjustments, from settling into a new home to finding the right places to shop. For newly appointed Fiji Pearls head coach Michelle Parsons, one of those first discoveries has been Extra Supermarket in Flagstaff, where she found familiar brands, great value, and a shopping experience that reminded her of home.
item.content:encoded:
MOVING to a new country comes with plenty of adjustments, from settling into a new home to finding the right places to shop.

For newly appointed Fiji Pearls head coach Michelle Parsons, one of those first discoveries has been Extra Supermarket in Flagstaff, where she found familiar brands, great value, and a shopping experience that reminded her of home.

Originally from Conifer Grove in Takanini, Auckland, Michelle recently relocated to Fiji to take up her role as Head Coach of the national netball team.

While her focus is on preparing the Fiji Pearls for future success, she has also been getting to know her new surroundings and that includes finding the perfect supermarket.

“I normally shop at Woolworths or New World in New Zealand, so I have been looking for a similar experience here in Suva,” Michelle said.

“Today I went shopping at Extra Supermarket and I really enjoyed the wide aisles, the layout was easy to navigate, and it stocked brands that I like.”

Michelle describes herself as a family-oriented person. She has been married to her husband, Graeme, for more than three decades.

“I am married to my best friend Graeme. We have been together for over 30-years and we have two children,” she said.

Back in New Zealand, the Parsons family leads a busy lifestyle balancing work, education and sport, while also caring for their much-loved German Shepherd.

Their weekly grocery bill generally ranges between NZ$600 ($F764.65) and NZ$700 ($F 892.09), making value for money an important part of every shopping trip.

“As a family of four, with a big German Shepherd dog, we would spend on average $600 to $700 a week,” Michelle explained.

“Here in Suva, I’ve spent approximately $200 per shop so far, which totals around $1,000 since I arrived.”

Health and nutrition have always been priorities in the Parsons household, especially with Michelle’s career in elite sport. She believes good food plays a major role in maintaining energy and wellbeing.

“As a family with busy lives filled with work, education and sport, we are very mindful of eating what we need to keep us thriving through the day,” she said.

“We make sure we have protein at all three meals, vegetables with at least two main meals, and fruit is always available.”

Of course, balance is equally important.

“We do enjoy treats,” she laughed. “Saturday is our treat day. During winter in New Zealand, I often make warm puddings using fruit as a base, but here in Fiji I’ve found yoghurt with berries or fresh watermelon as a lovely tropical substitute.

Although Michelle admits she was surprised by the cost of some groceries in Fiji, particularly fresh produce and meat, she was delighted by the bargains available at Extra Supermarket.

“Compared to New Zealand, I have been surprised at the cost of food, particularly fresh food and meat,” she said.

“But there were some fantastic deals at Extra that meant I could stock up on fresh broccoli and watermelon, as well as pantry staples like baked beans, tinned tomatoes and gluten-free pasta.

As someone who follows a gluten-free diet, Michelle was especially pleased to discover Extra’s dedicated gluten-free section.

“It was great to see a gluten-free area at Extra,” she said. “It’s wonderful knowing those options are available, and I also picked up yoghurt, Fiji Water and other everyday essentials.”

Her visit also uncovered products that support her active lifestyle.

“It was great to see those Extra stocks USL tape, which is really helpful for managing my knee injuries while I work out,” Michelle said.

“They also have excellent sports nutrition products like Musashi protein, pre-workout supplements and recovery drinks such as Up & Go and Athena Protein Water.

Like many families facing increasing living costs, Michelle says careful budgeting has become part of everyday life.

“We’re looking at alternative brands instead of sticking to our usual favourites,” she explained.

“We compare prices carefully and buy the best value. We’ve cut back on some treats, look for the best deals on chicken, fish and meat, and prepare dinners that are large enough to become lunch the next day. I also enjoy making sauces from scratch and I love baking.”

While she frequently visited local farmers’ markets in New Zealand, Michelle has yet to explore the Suva Municipal Market because of her demanding coaching schedule.

“I understand Saturday is the best day to visit, but that’s when I’m at netball all day,” she said. “When I do get the opportunity, I’ll definitely be looking for fresh green vegetables and fruit that I can prepare as healthy snacks.”

item.title: TIMES SHOPPER | Bring flair to your home
item.description: IN today’s fast-paced world, the home has become more than just a place to live it’s a space to relax, recharge, and reconnect with family. Whether you’re furnishing a new home or refreshing your current living space, choosing the right furniture can transform everyday living into a more comfortable and stylish experience. Refresh your home with Courts’ latest collection of quality furniture, feat
item.content:encoded:
IN today’s fast-paced world, the home has become more than just a place to live it’s a space to relax, recharge, and reconnect with family. Whether you’re furnishing a new home or refreshing your current living space, choosing the right furniture can transform everyday living into a more comfortable and stylish experience.

Refresh your home with Courts’ latest collection of quality furniture, featuring luxurious overseas recliner lounges, stylish dining sets, versatile sofa beds, and durable solid wood beds at exceptional prices. Designed to combine comfort, functionality, and contemporary style, the range offers something to suit every lifestyle and budget.

At the heart of the collection are thoughtfully engineered overseas lounges, designed with ergonomic comfort to provide exceptional support for everyday use. Whether you’re unwinding after a long day, enjoying a movie night, or spending quality time with family and friends, these lounges deliver a seating experience that enhances relaxation.

Available in a wide selection of premium materials including full leather, semi-leather, air leather, PVC leather, microfiber, polyester, premium fabrics, solid wood, and metal finishes, every piece is crafted for lasting durability, everyday comfort, and easy maintenance. With a variety of colours and designs to choose from, homeowners can effortlessly complement any interior décor.

The manual recliner function allows users to adjust their seating position smoothly and comfortably, while generous seating space ensures every member of the family can relax in style. Built to balance modern aesthetics with everyday practicality, these lounges are the perfect addition to contemporary homes.

Beyond lounges, the collection includes elegant dining suites for memorable family meals, practical sofa beds that maximise space for guests, and beautifully crafted solid wood beds designed to provide years of dependable comfort. Every piece reflects Courts’ commitment to quality craftsmanship and outstanding value.

Check out our stunning bedroom collection, featuring beautifully crafted solid wood beds designed for durability, comfort, and timeless style. Available in a range of modern and classic designs, each bed is built using quality materials to provide lasting support while adding warmth and elegance to your bedroom. Whether you’re furnishing a master suite, guest room, or children’s bedroom, there’s a design to suit every home and budget.

Complement your bedroom with Courts exquisite bedding range, offering premium mattresses, stylish bed linen, soft pillows, mattress protectors, and luxurious comforters for the ultimate sleeping experience. Carefully selected to provide superior comfort and support, the bedding collection helps create a restful sanctuary where you can enjoy quality sleep night after night.

Perfect for families, first-home buyers, and anyone looking to refresh their home, this furniture collection makes it easier than ever to create a living space you’ll love all at competitive prices.

With some of the best credit deals in town, Courts makes it easy for families across Fiji to bring home quality furniture without stretching their budgets. Flexible payment options allow more households to enjoy premium comfort and modern living while paying at a pace that suits them.

Visit your nearest Courts showroom today to explore the latest range of overseas lounges and quality furniture. Discover exceptional value, modern designs, and quality craftsmanship that will help transform your house into the home you’ve always wanted.

Stay connected with Courts by scanning the QR code for the latest promotions, OB and Red Pen Deal locations, and exclusive offers. You can also download the myCourts App to browse featured products, access your account, receive customer support, and stay up to date with the latest deals all from your mobile device.

Courts has a wide range of couches available in a selection of premium materials including full leather, semi-leather, air leather, PVC leather, microfiber, polyester, premium fabrics, solid wood, and metal finishes, with every piece crafted for lasting durability, everyday comfort, and easy maintenance. Pictures: SUPPLIED

Complement your bedroom with Courts exquisite bedding range, offering premium mattresses, stylish bed linen, soft pillows, mattress protectors, and luxurious comforters for the ultimate sleeping experience.

Refresh your home with Courts’ latest collection of quality furniture, featuring stylish dining sets at exceptional prices.

© 2026 FijiTimes. All rights reserved.

item.title: TIMES SHOPPER | Finding a taste of home
item.description: MOVING to a new country means adapting to a new way of life, and for newly appointed Fiji Pearls head coach Michelle Parsons, that journey has included finding a supermarket that feels like home. Having recently relocated from Conifer Grove in Auckland, New Zealand, Michelle says she was searching for a shopping experience similar to what she enjoyed back home.
item.content:encoded:
MOVING to a new country means adapting to a new way of life, and for newly appointed Fiji Pearls head coach Michelle Parsons, that journey has included finding a supermarket that feels like home.

Having recently relocated from Conifer Grove in Auckland, New Zealand, Michelle says she was searching for a shopping experience similar to what she enjoyed back home.

She found just that during her first visit to Extra Supermarket in Suva.

“I normally shop at Woolworths or New World in New Zealand, so I have been looking for a similar experience here in Suva,” Michelle said.

“I really enjoyed the wide aisles, the easy-to-navigate layout and the range of brands available.”A passionate advocate for healthy living, Michelle says nutrition plays an important role in her busy lifestyle, and she was impressed by Extra’s selection of fresh produce, gluten-free products and sports nutrition.

“There were some fantastic deals on fresh food and pantry staples,” she said.

“It was also great to see a dedicated gluten-free section and products that support an active lifestyle, including sports nutrition and recovery options.

“Although she admits she was surprised by the price of some groceries compared with New Zealand, Michelle says smart shopping and taking advantage of weekly specials make a real difference. Her advice to shoppers is simple.

“Look for the deals highlighted on the shelves because the savings are really worthwhile.”

© 2026 FijiTimes. All rights reserved.

item.title: TIMES SHOPPER | Bake cakes with Betty
item.description: IT all started in 1921 when a simple contest would give birth to an icon. The contest called on home cooks to solve a jigsaw puzzle for a chance to win a pincushion in the shape of a bag of gold medal flour.
item.content:encoded:
IT all started in 1921 when a simple contest would give birth to an icon.

The contest called on home cooks to solve a jigsaw puzzle for a chance to win a pincushion in the shape of a bag of gold medal flour.

Following this contest, the character Betty Crocker was created to give a personal response to an overwhelming number of letters from women constantly seeking cooking advice.

1. 1/2 cup Betty Crocker Rich and Creamy Chocolate Frosting;

3. 1 box Betty Crocker Super Moist Chocolate Fudge Cake Mix;

5. 1/3 cup finely chopped cocktail peanuts;

6. 1 container cool whipped frozen topping;

8. 2 tablespoons finely chopped cocktail peanuts;

1. Heat oven to 176°C. Spray cooking spray on a baking pan.

2. In a large microwaveable bowl, microwave 1/2 cup frosting for 5-10 seconds or until frosting is thin.

Beat milk with whisk until blended. Stir in dry cake mix, spread batter evenly in pan.

Sprinkle chocolate chips and 1/3 cup peanuts evenly on top

3. Bake for 28 to 32 minutes or until toothpick inserted in centre of the cake comes out almost clean.

Cool completely for 1 hour 30 minutes 4. When ready to serve, use a spoon to spread whipped topping over cooled cake.

Sprinkle chopped candy bars and 2 tablespoons peanuts on top.

In a small microwaveable bowl, microwave 1/2 cup frosting for 10-15 seconds or until frosting is thin.

Using a fork, drizzle the frosting over cake.

A product of General Mills, Betty Crocker is distributed in Fiji by the Motibhai Group – Sharing quality, sharing happiness.

Left: Mara Kapaiwai from Valelevu at CJS Supermarket Nabua.

Mouth-watering Betty Crocker cake . Pictures: SUPPLIED

© 2026 FijiTimes. All rights reserved.

item.title: LOCAL GETAWAYS | A peaceful getaway
item.description: Tucked away in the lush highlands between Nadi and Lautoka, Koroyanitu Heritage Park is one of Fiji’s hidden gems for nature lovers and adventure seekers. Home to spectacular waterfalls, panoramic mountain views and rich biodiversity, the community-managed park offers visitors the chance to experience Fiji beyond its beaches. Whether you’re looking for a leisurely walk-through native forest, a cha
item.content:encoded:
Tucked away in the lush highlands between Nadi and Lautoka, Koroyanitu Heritage Park is one of Fiji’s hidden gems for nature lovers and adventure seekers.

Home to spectacular waterfalls, panoramic mountain views and rich biodiversity, the community-managed park offers visitors the chance to experience Fiji beyond its beaches. Whether you’re looking for a leisurely walk-through native forest, a challenging climb to the summit of Mt Batilamu, or an overnight stay in a traditional village lodge, Koroyanitu combines breathtaking scenery with authentic Fijian culture and warm village hospitality. Koroyanitu Heritage Park near Nadi is home to some of Fiji’s most scenic hikes. Whether you opt for a day hike or an overnight trekking experience, the stunning landscape and rich Fijian culture make it truly memorable for a local getaway adventure and also for tourists.

Here is what www,fiji,travel website has to say:

The Park features three main trails – two shorter trails each to a different waterfall and a longer, more strenuous hike to the summit of Mt. Batilamu, Fiji’s fourth highest mountain (1,100m or 3,608ft).

Koroyanitu National Heritage Park is a stunning 35km sanctuary established in 1989 to protect Fiji’s unique flora and fauna. With waterfalls and camping spots including cozy lodges and breathtaking views out to the islands, Koroyanitu’s hiking trails are a must-do for any serious hiker.

Located just 15km or a 30-minute drive inland from Lautoka City, Abaca Village is the ‘base camp’ for your adventure.

The dirt road to the village can get a little rough during the rainy season, so a 4WD truck or a arranged taxi familiar with the route is the best way to get there.

All tracks kick off at the Visitor Centre in Abaca Village.

They’ll provide you with trail maps, expert advice on the best routes, updates on current conditions and can also arrange homestays, village lunches and a personal guide if you want.

Also let them know If you’re a keen birder in need of a knowledgeable guide.

The Park is one of the best birdwatching spots in Fiji and home to rare birds like the Masked Shining Parrot, Fiji Parrotfinch, Pacific Harrier, Red Avadavat, and the Fiji White-eye.

Rather than do it all in one day, you can break up your hike and explore the Park at a more leisurely pace if you stay overnight in a village homestay or one of two rustic lodges.

Nase Lodge, a 40 minute walk from the village, is the perfect base to explore Koroyanitu’s waterfall hikes.

But for a truly unforgettable experience, camp out in the lodge at the top of Mt. Batilamu, where you’ll be treated to a breathtaking sunset and sunrise over Nadi and the nearby islands.

You can then continue your hike down to the village the next day.

Both lodges sleep multiple people and have basic cooking and toilet facilities.

As the hike starts off in Abaca Village, you should bring a sulu (sarong) to wear over your pants within the village boundaries.

A small sevusevu (kava bundle) is also respectful but not essential.

If you need, you can buy this from the Lautoka food market before you head inland.

Like any remote hike, pack plenty of water, energy-boosting snacks, and lightweight, long-sleeved gear to tackle the sun, mosquitoes, and long grass.

Don’t forget sturdy shoes, a map or GPS, and some insect repellent.

Sunscreen and a hat will keep you protected, and a rain jacket will ensure you’re prepared for any unexpected showers.

If you’re staying overnight in the lodges, make sure to cozy up in warm layers (it can get chilly) and bring basic ingredients to cook an easy dinner and breakfast, a flashlight and a deck of playing cards for entertainment.

Also bring some swimmers and a spare change of clothes if you intend to swim in any of the waterfalls.

Start early to catch the wildlife and skip the midday heat, and don’t forget plenty of water and snacks.

We also recommend taking a local guide, especially on the longer hike up Mount Batilamu for extra safety on the trail and their excellent knowledge of local plants and animals.

The longer mountain trail, though well-trodden, does not have toilet facilities or any places to rest and some parts can get slippery in rainy weather.

Please also note that there are no handrails along the track, so you are walking at your own risk.

The villagers are keen conservationists and work closely with environmental NGOs.

As a community run eco-tourism activity, a portion of your hiking fee also helps support education, healthcare, and sustainable development in Abaca Village.

item.title: TIMES SHOPPER | Agnes maintains healthy meals at home
item.description: TIMES SHOPPER | Agnes maintains healthy meals at home
